reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome (no-GPU) end-to-end across all 5 pages; desktop+mobile no-overflow, light+dark screenshots read

rubric.evidence: Source calls a REAL WebLLM path: worker.js imports createEngine + streamChat from lib/webllm.js and streams via engine.chat.completions.create({stream:true}); the page gates on webGPUAdapterAvailable() before load. Verified the MLC id Phi-4-mini-instruct-q4f16_1-MLC EXISTS in @mlc-ai/web-llm 0.2.84 prebuiltAppConfig (esm.run serves 0.2.84) — vram_required_MB 3437.58, context_window_size 4096. In the no-GPU headless runner all 4 single-model pages reached the honest 'unsupported / needs-WebGPU (no GPU adapter here)' state — NEVER a faked token.
rubric.notes: Real streamed token output on a GPU device was not observable in this no-GPU sandbox by design (WebLLM is WebGPU-only, no WASM fallback). The load path and MLC id are verified; a GPU-device run to observe live decode + tok/s is the open item.

rubric.evidence: Expected shape is a streamed instruction-tuned chat continuation. Basics streams a single answer; practical constrains output to strict JSON via response_format:{type:'json_object'} and runs JSON.parse() then checks the chosen tool is one of the declared tools (a real function-calling round-trip Phi-4-mini is trained for); wild streams numbered step-by-step reasoning + an 'Answer:' line; multi-model feeds retrieved passages and expects a grounded, citation-tagged answer. See-inside shows generated tokens, ttft, decode ms, tok/s, and the exact messages array (wire).
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity of the tool-routing JSON and grounded RAG answer is verified in code + the wire panel; observing the exact model output requires a GPU device (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (microsoft/Phi-4-mini-instruct linked to card), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4f16_1), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance (read in the desktop screenshot) lists model, MLC build Phi-4-mini-instruct-q4f16_1-MLC, runtime WebLLM/MLC (not Transformers.js), params 3.8B q4f16_1, what's-new-vs-Phi-3.5 (200k vocab, GQA, function calling), backend WebGPU-only ~3.4 GB VRAM, download ~2.2 GB, context 4096, license MIT.
rubric.notes: Honest, complete disclosure; matches exactly what worker.js loads (MODEL_ID Phi-4-mini-instruct-q4f16_1-MLC via createEngine).

rubric.evidence: Every page uses the shared createModelLoader (no bespoke Load button). WebGPU gate fires first: on the no-GPU runner the single-model pages land in 'unsupported' with the honest needs-WebGPU message and NO action button, and the fallback panel explains how to enable WebGPU + the ~2.2 GB cost — no silent download. The multi-model page's MiniLM loader (WASM, no GPU) correctly shows the 'download-required' (absent) state while the Phi loader shows needs-WebGPU. Per-model 'Clear cached model' control is provided by the shared loader once ready.
rubric.notes: On a GPU device the state matrix (absent→Download, current→auto-init no button, partial→Re-download) is driven by the same shared loader as the other WebLLM pages; the needs-WebGPU honest gate is what this environment can prove.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present on every page), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader), webgpu-capability-honest (real adapter probe → labelled unsupported). Driven runs: 0 uncaught JS errors, 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests on all 5 pages at desktop AND mobile. Generation failures set status .err with the message; Stop calls engine.interruptGenerate().
rubric.notes: Honest needs-WebGPU rendered on all pages. Offline-cached / partial-evicted state drives need a GPU device with a warm cache (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main: system-prompt textarea, temp/top_p/max-tokens ranges with live value bind, message textarea, Send/Stop/Clear. Basics: example chips + Ask. Practical: tools panel + example chips + request textarea + Route, with live JSON.parse + tool-name validity. Wild: persona chips (aria-pressed) + puzzle chips + Ask. Multi-model: two shared loaders + KB textarea + question. Every control is a real labelled element; chips ≥40px, composer buttons ≥44px min-block-size.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced via rendered screenshots; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus, Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter) on a GPU device is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run every answer/output area shows only a muted placeholder; on the no-GPU runner the loader shows the honest unsupported state and NO tokens are ever synthesised. There is no canned reply path anywhere — output only ever comes from engine.chat.completions.create.
rubric.notes: Verified across all 5 pages: no pre-baked result, and the WebGPU gate prevents any output when the model genuinely can't run.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (no content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; chat log + answer areas aria-live=polite; textareas have aria-label; example/persona chip sets grouped with role=group aria-label; persona chips use aria-pressed. Design-system focus styles inherited from public/styles.css.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming answer on a GPU device would further validate.

rubric.evidence: Driven matrix: documentElement.scrollWidth - clientWidth == 0 (no horizontal overflow) at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on ALL 5 pages. Screenshots read: phi-4 main (desktop light + dark) — at-a-glance table wraps, lede clean; smollm-style mobile at 360px — heading + lede wrap, no overflow. min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere on bubbles/flex items; grids use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr); glance cells overflow-wrap:anywhere.
rubric.notes: Support recorded via responsive-check seed; the driven no-overflow pass across desktop+mobile + light/dark reads is strong evidence.

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js, new Worker(url,{type:'module'})) so decode never blocks the main thread; tokens post incrementally via postMessage and the UI only appends text. conformance worker-compliance=source-pass, inference-off-main-thread=pass. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture); driven runs stayed responsive with 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while streaming on a GPU device would confirm; the off-thread worker + incremental streaming is the key mitigation, matching the reference WebLLM pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream LLM tokens incrementally web worker without blocking main thread INP
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread and yield/stream incrementally so it never becomes a long task that spikes INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in models/phi-4-webllm/worker.js; the worker posts small deltas the main thread just appends. No synchronous main-thread loop over tokens.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance worker-compliance + inference-off-main-thread pass; driven runs stayed responsive with 0 console errors on all 5 pages

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel form controls no horizontal overflow narrow mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + gap + intrinsic sizing and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content; use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr) so columns stack instead of overflowing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips use flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles + composer textarea set min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; the practical + multi-model grids use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr); glance cells overflow-wrap:anywhere.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: scrollWidth-clientWidth==0 at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages (driven headless)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status streaming updates label every control
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region, label every control, group related controls, and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status; aria-live=polite chat log + answer; aria-label on textareas; role=group on chip sets; persona chips use aria-pressed; caret animation disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass; DOM inspection + rendered screenshots

guidanceConsulted.query: textarea that grows to fit content field-sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use field-sizing:content with min/max block-size so composer textareas grow with input instead of scrolling.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (shared CHAT_CSS) — .composer textarea and .sysbox textarea use field-sizing:content with min/max block-size caps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: CHAT_CSS in models/phi-4-webllm/webllm-chat.js; no overflow observed on mobile

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); every page links the shared stylesheet and crumbs back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs present on all 5 pages (read in screenshots)

openQuestions: On a real WebGPU device: observe live streamed decode + tok/s in the see-inside panel, and confirm the practical page's tool-routing JSON parses and selects a valid tool across the example requests.
Confirm Phi-4-mini's function-calling reliability in JSON mode on-device (does it consistently emit {tool, arguments} matching a declared tool).
Keyboard-only traversal + visible focus across all 5 pages on a GPU device (Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter, persona chips, sliders).
Drive the warm-cache auto-init (no button) + partial/evicted + offline-cached states on a GPU device with the ~2.2 GB model cached.

followUpGoals.goal: GPU-device verification pass for phi-4-webllm: capture live decode/tok/s, the tool-routing JSON output, and the RAG grounded answer as retained evidence.
Add a Phi-4-mini multi-turn tool-calling demo that actually dispatches the emitted call to a mock function and feeds the result back (a full agent loop).
Add a side-by-side Phi-3.5 vs Phi-4 comparison demo (same prompt, same sampling) to make the generational difference legible.

summary: phi-4-webllm is a strong, honest, genuinely-distinct demo: the newest Phi generation (Phi-4-mini-instruct 3.8B, verified present in @mlc-ai/web-llm 0.2.84 prebuiltAppConfig as Phi-4-mini-instruct-q4f16_1-MLC, vram 3437.58 MB, ctx 4096) on WebLLM/WebGPU, distinct from the built Phi-3.5-mini page (new tokenizer + function-calling focus). Its practical rung is a real on-device tool-routing / function-calling demo (JSON mode → {tool, arguments} validated against declared tools), differentiating it from Phi-3.5's schema extraction. Driven end-to-end headless (no-GPU): honest needs-WebGPU state on every single-model page (no faked tokens), the MiniLM half of the RAG page shows the honest absent/download state, 0 JS/console errors, 0 failed requests, and no horizontal overflow at 1280x800 or 360x740 across all 5 pages, light + dark both legible. Real streamed decode is verified by source + MLC-id existence; observing live tokens needs a GPU device (the high-priority follow-up). Revision 1.
